verdict_reason: The disagreement entry on sthāṇu / acala / sanātana names a real philological-doctrinal stake. Translations conflate sthāṇu and acala into a single 'unchanging/immovable' pair, losing the distinction between 'fixed because there is nowhere else to be' (sthāṇu, structural-stability via stake/stump imagery) and 'not subject to motion' (acala, no-displacement). The explanation captures three distinct facts: (a) translations elide the distinction, (b) Shankara reads the eight properties as a deductive chain terminating in sanātana as temporal-capstone, (c) Ramanuja reads them as eight angles on subtlety/pervasion. Both readings are preserved as compatible-but-different. Score 9, PASS.
